articles.title: ICE's growing detention footprint, and the communities fighting back
articles.summary: Resistance in both Democratic and Republican cities points to broader unease with the direction of immigration enforcement.

articles.title: With more older drivers on the road, states try to balance safety and mobility
articles.summary: The number of older drivers on the road is climbing. Safety advocates want tougher rules for relicensing, but many drivers say they shouldn't be forced to give up their mobility because of age alone.

articles.title: What does a 'GLP-1 Friendly' diet look like? We asked nutritionists
articles.summary: Big food companies are starting to market to people on the powerful new obesity meds with labels that say "GLP-1 Friendly." Nutritionists help us decode that message.

articles.title: Worried about a shaky stock market? This is what financial advisers suggest you do
articles.summary: Their answer depends on how soon you need to tap into your funds — and it might simply be "do nothing."

articles.title: As D.C.'s cherry blossom trees near peak bloom, here's a guide to their history
articles.summary: The renowned trees along Washington, D.C's Tidal Basin were sent as a gift from Japan in 1912. Some of the original trees are still there.

articles.title: New York's LaGuardia Airport closed after jet collides with firefighting vehicle
articles.summary: An Air Canada regional jet struck a fire truck on the runway after landing at New York's LaGuardia Airport late Sunday night. Two people were killed, according to a person familiar with the investigat

articles.title: Ukrainians are growing more pessimistic about the prospects for peace, survey says
articles.summary: As the Iran war delays peace talks for Ukraine -- and global supplies of air defense missiles -- there are signs that fewer Ukrainians are willing to endure Russia's war for as long as necessary.

articles.title: ICE officers set to deploy to airports as delays mount, border czar Homan confirms
articles.summary: Border czar Tom Homan says ICE agents will help the Transportation Security Administration "move those lines" while also enforcing immigration law.

articles.title: Iranians Fear Trump’s Threat to Strike Power Plants
articles.summary: With President Trump threatening to strike Iranian power plants, some war-weary civilians are panicking over a possible new debacle.

articles.title: How Obama’s Strategist Discouraged Biden From Running in 2016
articles.summary: In a new set of oral histories, David Plouffe, President Barack Obama’s political adviser, described how he urged Vice President Joseph R. Biden Jr. not to challenge Hillary Clinton for the nomination

articles.title: Israel’s Missile Defense Under Scrutiny After Iranian Attack
articles.summary: Two missiles landed hours apart, wreaking havoc in two towns near a heavily guarded nuclear site in the Negev Desert.

articles.title: ICE to Help TSA at Airports Amid Partial Shutdown, Trump’s Border Czar Says
articles.summary: Tom Homan, President Trump’s chief border official, cast the operation largely as one to help ease long security lines. The Homeland Security Department said 14 airports across the country would be in

articles.title: How the G.O.P. and Democrats Are Talking About the Surge in Gas Prices
articles.summary: Republicans have largely been on defense against Democratic criticism. Here are five arguments advanced by figures in both parties.

articles.title: Israel Thought It Could Spur Rebellion Inside Iran. That Hasn’t Happened.
articles.summary: President Trump’s hopes that an Israeli plan to ignite an internal uprising against Iran’s theocratic government could bring the war to a swift end have so far been dashed.

articles.title: For ‘Buffy’ Fans, Nicholas Brendon’s Xander Was a Complicated Everyman
articles.summary: The actor, who died on Friday, was a fan favorite on “Buffy the Vampire Slayer,” even as his character mixed quiet heroism with hostility toward the show’s women.

articles.title: Italy's far-right Prime Minister's political future at stake in referendum vote
articles.summary: Italian Prime Minister's close relationship with President Trump may weigh on a referendum on reforming Italy's judiciary.

articles.title: NATO allies prepare for a Russian attack in the far North
articles.summary: Despite tensions, U.S. and other NATO nations conduct exercises in the far North off Norway to prepare for Russian attack.

articles.title: Israel's war strategy involves high-level assassinations. But will it win the war?
articles.summary: The war between the US, Israel and Iran is intensifying. NPR's Adrian Ma speaks with Yossi Melman, who reports on Israeli intelligence, about the state of the conflict as it enters its fourth week.

articles.title: What a team of investigative reporters found out about Banksy's identity
articles.summary: A Reuters investigative team discovered intriguing details about Banksy's identity after seeing his art pop up in Ukraine.

articles.title: This lab that's determined to discover new drugs isn't where you might expect
articles.summary: A scientist from Zambia who loves — LOVES! — chemistry runs a lab in South Africa that is being hailed for "extraordinary" work.

articles.title: The effects of the Iran war on environmental and human health, according to an expert
articles.summary: As the war in Iran enters its fourth week, the costs are adding up. NPR's Ayesha Rascoe talks to Doug Weir, with the Conflict and War Observatory, about impacts to human health and the environment.

articles.title: Trump threatens Iran's power plants as war enters fourth week with no end in sight
articles.summary: We have the latest on the U-S and Israeli war on Iran, where in the past 48 hours, Israel has struck one of Iran's nuclear facilities and Iran has responded with strikes in Israel.

articles.title: Trump threatens to 'obliterate' Iran's power plants as Iran strikes 2 Israeli cities
articles.summary: Iran launched missiles at two southern Israeli cities that lie close to the country's main nuclear research center, while President Trump gave Iran 48 hours to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Nicholas Brendon, Beloved Sidekick on ‘Buffy the Vampire Slayer,’ Dies at 54
articles.summary: He played the part of Xander Harris, one of Buffy’s closest friends, on the hit television show about a teenage girl who protects the world from monsters.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
